gov.memory.title: Memory Sovereignty
gov.memory.desc: Always query qmd agent-memory before writing new knowledge. Reuse and update existing files before generating duplicate ones.
gov.lesson.title: Lesson Curation Gate
gov.lesson.desc: Lessons require logical, objective validation evidence. Rubber-stamping is impossible: graduate.py strictly requires a --rationale flag.
gov.daemon.title: Launchd Daemon Stack
gov.daemon.desc: Four launchd jobs plus local Ollama manage search, watchers, dashboards, auto-learning, and phi4-mini inference locally.
gov.auto.title: Autonomous Learning
gov.auto.desc: Auto-learn runs silently in the background every 30 minutes, keeping cognitive state up to date without adding latency or developer interruption.
footer.lesson: Engineering Hour · Interactive bonus for Part 11 · Living Memory
footer.note: © 2026 Paulo Griiettner · Single-file interactive blueprint for the Agent Brain system · Trigger the flows on the left to watch packets move through it.
dyn.node.clientClaude.title: Claude Code Agent
dyn.node.clientClaude.desc: Claude Code is an external AI agent tool integrated with the workspace. To prevent context lock-in, every setting routes to the centralized, tool-agnostic `.agent/` folder through redirects.
dyn.node.clientClaude.extra: Keeps startup files clean and prevents tool vendor lock-in. Claude Code loads .agent/AGENTS.md and state/STATE.md to initiate context.
dyn.node.clientCursor.title: Cursor IDE Agent
dyn.node.clientCursor.desc: Uses Cursor MDC custom rules. Configured to apply globally to all tasks, routing context parsing to the centralized .agent/ architecture.
dyn.node.clientCursor.extra: alwaysApply: true is enabled so Cursor constantly refers back to the Agent Brain structure without human prompting.
dyn.node.clientTerminal.title: Terminal / Warp / Oz
dyn.node.clientTerminal.desc: Launches local CLI tasks and direct MCP search queries. Links the operating framework back to system workflows through customized aliases.
dyn.node.clientTerminal.extra: A symlink couples terminal capabilities directly into the embedded qmd engine so knowledge is reachable from any terminal context.
dyn.node.gatewayAgents.title: AGENTS.md Navigation Index
dyn.node.gatewayAgents.desc: The centralized entry point for all AI sessions across every client. It orchestrates active project skills, agents, rules, and states with minimal token overhead.
dyn.node.gatewayAgents.extra: Aggressive constraint design keeps root startup load around 500 tokens. Detailed content is fetched on demand through search queries.
dyn.node.serviceQmd.title: qmd MCP Vector Search
dyn.node.serviceQmd.desc: Local vector search engine that manages structural embeddings. It indexes source files and curated memory, connecting directly with IDE tools through Model Context Protocol (MCP).
dyn.node.serviceQmd.extra: Runs on port 8181 for MCP and 8182 for the dashboard. Git hooks and WatchPaths trigger incremental reindexing.
dyn.node.serviceOllama.title: Local Ollama API
dyn.node.serviceOllama.desc: Local inference platform for AI. Runs tasks without sending proprietary code or design strategy outside the local disk.
dyn.node.serviceOllama.extra: Configured with keep_alive: 0 in API calls. Phi4-mini unloads from CPU/RAM immediately after responding, consuming 0GB at idle.
dyn.node.gatewayState.title: STATE.md & Commit Hooks
dyn.node.gatewayState.desc: Durable session tracker that logs active development state. It pairs with a post-commit hook to create pending analysis context.
dyn.node.gatewayState.extra: Writes pending-analysis.md with change details. The agent processes and removes it at the start of the next workflow.
dyn.node.storageSkills.title: Procedural Skills & Agent Roles
dyn.node.storageSkills.desc: Defines modular on-demand procedures and roles including memory-steward, conversation-digest, and reflect. Prompts are not preloaded, keeping sessions lean.
dyn.node.storageSkills.extra: Activated only when explicit trigger phrases such as "learn from this" or "digest session" are detected.
dyn.node.storageTools.title: CLI Lifecycle Tools
dyn.node.storageTools.desc: A collection of Python scripts that operate directly on the lesson database files. Structured for robust processing without external dependencies.
dyn.node.storageTools.extra: Uses only the Python standard library. Zero pip dependencies keep execution stable across environments.
dyn.node.storageMemory.title: Curated Durable Memory
dyn.node.storageMemory.desc: Stores long-term lesson archives, patterns, and framework conventions. Candidate and rejected pools preserve decision rationale.
dyn.node.storageMemory.extra: Saves project domain logic in memory/ and AI architecture knowledge in memory/brain/, preventing cross-contamination between knowledge bases.

dyn.cli.stage.output:
[STAGE] Lesson staged for review.
ID: candidate-d4b2
Claim: "Avoid global state in Drupal hooks"
Source: "code-review"
Status: Staged under .agent/memory/lessons/candidates/

dyn.cli.recall.output:
[RECALL] Searching Jaccard lexical match over 38 graduated lessons...

Match 1 [Score: 0.86]:
"Always use connection pooling for FastAPI DB"
Rationale: Observed connection exhaustion under load.

Match 2 [Score: 0.61]:
"Never inline SQL in FastAPI routes"
Rationale: Keep DB layer separated to preserve clean routing architectural pattern.
dyn.cli.graduate.output:
[GRADUATE] Candidate candidate-d4b2 successfully verified!
ID promoted: lesson-7e11
Claim: "Avoid global state in Drupal hooks"
Rationale: "proven across three decoupled hooks"
Status: Moved from candidates/ to lessons.jsonl

dyn.flow.auto.text: DAEMON: Scans Git activity ➜ Sends to local Ollama ➜ Dispatches to stage.py/learn.py
dyn.flow.reflect.text: SESSION END: reflect skill parses STATE.md ➜ Writes dated reflection ➜ Re-indexes qmd
dyn.flow.digest.text: DIGEST: Gathers context ➜ Filters duplicates with qmd ➜ Saves to Memory Layer
dyn.flow.context.text: RECOVERY: Reads index ➜ Captures pending context ➜ Queries qmd for task memory
dyn.flow.defaultStatus: SELECT PROTOCOL TO START STREAM SIMULATION
